spec: offices/pm/inbox/2026-04-29-from-spool-inverted-power-drill-findings-and-us235-correction.md (B-049/B-050 root causes from 2026-04-29 inverted drill) + offices/pm/backlog/B-047-pi-self-update-from-server-release-registry.md (US-B/C/D after US-A shipped Sprint 19) + offices/pm/tech_debt/TD-036/TD-039/TD-032/TD-033 (cleanup tier)
backlogItem: B-049 (drive_detect idle-poll, US-242) + B-050 (PowerMonitor activation, US-243) + B-047 US-B/C/D (server release registry + Pi update-checker + auto-apply, US-246/247/248) + TD-036/TD-032/TD-033/TD-039 cleanup

testBaseline.note: Post-Sprint-19 close baseline. main@5025508 (V0.19.0). Verify exact count at sprint start.

sprintNotes: GOAL: Sprint 19 fixed the trigger-source + detection bugs in US-216, but two architectural gaps remain that prevent staged shutdown from actually firing: (a) PowerMonitor never instantiated -> power_log empty across 5 drain tests (B-050); (b) drive_detect idle-poll never escalates on engine-on -> silent ECU-data loss when B-043 wiring lands (B-049). Sprint 20's first 4 stories close those gaps + add an e2e integration test that proves the full pipeline. Stories 5-7 advance B-047 self-update (US-A baseline shipped Sprint 19) toward a working update flow. Stories 8-10 close 3 open TDs (TD-036 BT-connect blocking, TD-032 dead BatteryMonitor remnant, TD-033 telemetry-logger UPS plumbing audit) + TD-039 process retro.
EXECUTION ORDER (deps-aware): TIER 1 no deps -- US-242, US-243, US-244, US-246, US-249, US-250, US-251. TIER 2 deps -- US-245 (deps US-243), US-247 (deps US-246), US-248 (deps US-247). 7 T1 + 3 T2.

THEME: Sprint 19 fixed the visible US-216 + US-228 bugs; Sprint 20 fixes the invisible architectural gaps that gate them actually working in production. After Sprint 20 + the next drain test + the next cold-start drive, US-216 should fire end-to-end for the first time across 6+ drain tests; every key-on (post-B-043 wiring) should reliably produce drive_summary + ECU data without manual intervention; Pi self-update should be one signed-off-by-CIO step away from working.

CONSTRAINTS (per feedback_runtime_validation_required.md): synthetic tests for runtime-only-verifiable stories (US-242, US-243, US-244, US-245) MUST tightly mock at hardware/lifecycle-signal level. US-242 mocks BATTERY_V trace at sensor-read level; US-243 mocks UpsMonitor.transitionDetected callback; US-244 mocks BT-connect timeout in lifecycle. Live drills (drain test 5, cold-start drive 6, B-043 wiring rollout) are POST-SPRINT ACTION ITEMS per CIO no-human-tasks rule.

GROUNDING SOURCES: 2026-04-29 Spool inverted-power drill note + 2026-04-30 Sprint 19 closure (US-235 audit confirmed CRATE disabled, US-237 v0004 closed TD-038) + B-049/B-050 backlog items + B-047 backlog item + TD-036/TD-032/TD-033/TD-039 records.

POST-SPRINT-20 ACTION ITEMS (NOT in sprint.json per CIO rule): (1) CIO runs drain test 5 with US-242+US-243 deployed; verify stage transitions fire AND power_log + battery_health_log populate. (2) CIO runs cold-start drive 6 with US-242+US-244 deployed; verify drive_start fires within seconds of engine-on AND drive_summary metadata non-NULL. (3) Marcus redeploys server with US-246 release registry endpoint. (4) Spool runs review ritual against drives 6+. (5) Marcus bumps deploy/RELEASE_VERSION at sprint close per feedback_pm_sprint_close_version_bump.md (V0.19.0 -> V0.20.0 expected; minor bump for new self-update sub-system + new orchestrator paths).

stories.title: drive_detect idle-poll -> active-poll escalation on engine-on detection (B-049)
stories.intent: During Spool's 2026-04-29 inverted-power drill, the engine ran ~1 minute with full alternator-charging signature in BATTERY_V (14.4V at idle, cranking dip 11.4V), BT/OBD adapter connected throughout, but drive_start NEVER FIRED -- zero ECU PIDs captured. Root cause: orchestrator's idle-poll mode only queries BATTERY_V (ELM_VOLTAGE / ATRV adapter-level, ECU-independent). drive_detector waits for ECU-dependent PIDs to start responding, but those PIDs are never queried in idle-poll. Critical: post-B-043 wiring, every key-on = Pi power-on -> Pi cold-boots -> idle-poll -> engine runs whole drive -> drive_start never fires -> ZERO ECU DATA captured. Silent data-loss bug that activates the moment B-043 wiring lands. Fix: when BATTERY_V > 13.8V sustained for N consecutive samples (alternator-active signature), escalate idle-poll to active-poll AND inject single-shot ECU probe of RPM PID. Drive 5 baseline confirms this car's alternator hits 14.4V on bulk-charge, so 13.8V cleanly distinguishes engine-on from any engine-off state.

filesToTouch: src/pi/obdii/orchestrator/core.py (idle-poll loop: when BATTERY_V > engineOnVoltageThreshold for engineOnSampleCount consecutive samples, escalate to active-poll AND inject single-shot RPM probe)
config.json pi.obdii.orchestrator: add engineOnVoltageThreshold=13.8, engineOnSampleCount=3
src/common/config/validator.py DEFAULTS for new keys

tests/pi/obdii/orchestrator/test_idle_poll_escalation.py (NEW -- mocks BATTERY_V trace [12.7,12.7,11.4,14.4,14.4,14.4] simulating engine-off->cranking->alternator-on; asserts orchestrator transitions idle->active by sample 6; asserts RPM probe injected; asserts drive_start fires after RPM response)
tests/pi/obdii/orchestrator/test_idle_poll_no_false_escalation.py (NEW -- mocks BATTERY_V stable at 12.7V with single 13.5V noise spike; asserts orchestrator stays idle; no probe fires)

13.8V threshold sustained 3 samples = alternator-active signature
Engine ran ~1 min during inverted drill, drive_start never fired (0 ECU rows)
Synthetic test must mock at sensor read level (getBatteryVoltage), not at orchestrator state level

If existing active-poll tests fail after the new escalation path is added -- STOP, revisit
If 13.8V threshold generates false positives in CIO drive testing -- STOP and pivot to fix-shape (b) periodic ECU probe; do not chase per-vehicle threshold tuning

stories.title: PowerMonitor DB-write path activation -- UpsMonitor -> power_log -> US-216 (B-050)
stories.intent: Architectural root cause of why US-216 staged shutdown has never fired across 5 drain tests despite Sprint 19's correct trigger source (US-234 SOC->VCELL) and correct BATTERY-detection (US-235). UpsMonitor IS alive and detects transitions correctly (8 transitions logged tonight). PowerMonitor (783 lines, writes to power_log + maintains state US-216 reads) IS NEVER INSTANTIATED in production -- enabled=false default + no orchestrator code path instantiates it. power_log table empty since installation. Sprint 19's US-234+US-235 work delivers CORRECT trigger source + CORRECT detection rules, but to a NON-EXISTENT listener. After this story, UpsMonitor transitions reach power_log and US-216 staged shutdown can actually fire on the next drain test.

stories.title: TD-036 close: orchestrator non-blocking BT-connect at lifecycle startup
stories.intent: Per TD-036 (filed Sprint 18 post-deploy): when Pi boots with engine off, orchestrator's _initializeConnection blocks indefinitely in the connect-retry loop because adapter responds but ECU is silent. runLoop never starts -> US-226 auto-sync (in runLoop) never fires. Currently a benign issue (CIO bench-state) but becomes critical post-B-043 wiring: every key-on cold-boots Pi, and if the engine takes >6 connect-retry-cycles to respond on K-line, US-226 auto-sync gets locked out for that drive. Fix: refactor _initializeConnection to be non-blocking after first wall-clock-bounded attempt (e.g., 30s); allow runLoop to start with connection in 'pending' state; runLoop's existing health-check + reconnect path handles late-arriving ECU response. US-226 auto-sync (interval-based) fires on schedule independent of OBD connection state.

filesToTouch: src/pi/obdii/orchestrator/lifecycle.py (refactor _initializeConnection to time-bound the initial connect attempt -- e.g., 30s wall-clock; on timeout, log + return without raising; runLoop starts with connection in pending state)
src/pi/obdii/orchestrator/core.py (runLoop: tolerate connection.state=PENDING; existing reconnect path handles transition to CONNECTED)
config.json pi.obdii.orchestrator: add initialConnectTimeoutSec=30
src/common/config/validator.py DEFAULTS for new key

tests/pi/obdii/orchestrator/test_lifecycle_initial_connect_timeout.py (NEW -- mocks ObdConnection.connect() blocking forever; asserts _initializeConnection returns within timeout; asserts runLoop starts; asserts US-226 _maybeTriggerIntervalSync fires on schedule)
tests/pi/obdii/orchestrator/test_lifecycle.py (UPDATE -- existing happy-path tests still pass)

stories.title: Power-mgmt e2e integration test -- UpsMonitor -> PowerMonitor -> Orchestrator -> ShutdownHandler
stories.intent: Sprint 19 + Sprint 20 deliver fixes across 4 power-mgmt modules: UpsMonitor (US-235 detection), PowerMonitor (US-243 wiring), PowerDownOrchestrator (US-234 trigger), ShutdownHandler (US-216 unchanged). No single test exercises the full pipeline. This story adds an e2e integration test that mocks MAX17048 register reads at the lowest level (I2cClient.readWord per US-235's pattern), simulates a full drain trace 4.20V -> 3.40V over mocked time, and asserts: BATTERY transitions detected -> power_log rows written -> orchestrator state machine progresses NORMAL->WARNING->IMMINENT->TRIGGER -> battery_health_log row created at WARNING -> systemctl poweroff invoked at TRIGGER. This is the synthetic equivalent of CIO running drain test 5 -- if it passes, drain test 5 is a high-confidence ship.
dependencies: US-243 (PowerMonitor must be instantiated for the integration path to exist)

stories.title: B-047 US-B: Server release registry endpoint (current version + history)
stories.intent: B-047 (Pi self-update from server release registry) shipped US-A in Sprint 19 (deploy versioning baseline V0.18.0). US-B is the server-side endpoint that publishes the current authoritative version. After this lands, Pi update-checker (US-C) can poll the server, compare its own deploy/RELEASE_VERSION to the server's current, and decide whether to update. Endpoint: GET /api/v1/release/current returns {version, releasedAt, gitHash, description}. Bonus: GET /api/v1/release/history returns last N releases for debugging. Auth: existing X-API-Key middleware (US-CMP-002). Reads source-of-truth from server-side .deploy-version file (written by deploy-server.sh per US-241 contract).

stories.title: B-047 US-C: Pi update-checker daemon -- poll server registry, decide if update needed
stories.intent: Pi-side daemon that periodically (configurable interval, default 1 hour) polls the server's GET /api/v1/release/current endpoint, compares the server's version to the Pi's local deploy/RELEASE_VERSION, and decides whether an update is needed. If update available, writes a marker file (e.g., /var/lib/eclipse-obd/update-pending.json) with target-version + downloaded-from URL + rationale; the actual apply step (US-248) reads this marker. Daemon honors safety preconditions: do NOT check for updates while a drive is in progress (drive_id != None) -- drive-state is sacred. Runs as part of orchestrator runLoop or as a separate systemd timer (Marcus + Rex pick during grooming).
dependencies: US-246 (server endpoint must exist before Pi can poll it)

stories.title: B-047 US-D: Pi auto-update apply -- download + install + verify (rollback on failure)
stories.intent: Pi-side apply step: when US-247 has written /var/lib/eclipse-obd/update-pending.json, this story's UpdateApplier reads it, downloads the new release artifact (git fetch + checkout to a deploy-staged worktree), runs deploy-pi.sh in dry-run mode to validate, then commits (git stash old version, apply new). On any failure: rollback to prior version + clear marker + log + alert. Safety preconditions (same as US-247): skip if drive in progress; skip if power source = BATTERY (don't update mid-shutdown); skip if connection_log shows recent OBD activity within last 5 min.

stories.title: TD-039 close: server schema-migration discipline -- PRD template + CI schema-diff check
stories.intent: Per TD-039: Sprint 16 US-213 shipped the server schema migration gate, but it has been silently a no-op for tables where Pi schema diverges from server schema (e.g., drive_summary -- TD-038 closed only because Sprint 19 US-237 explicitly authored v0004; the gate did not detect the drift). Process gap: no rule enforces 'every Pi-schema-changing PR includes a server-migration partner.' Two-part fix: (a) PRD template field 'Server migration required: yes/no/N/A' so grooming surfaces the question; (b) CI script that diffs Pi sqlite schema vs server mariadb schema for tables present on both, flags any column-set divergence at PR time.

filesToTouch: offices/pm/prds/_template.md (UPDATE -- add 'Server migration required: yes / no / N/A' checkbox at top)

scripts/schema_diff.py (NEW -- reads Pi sqlite schema (src/pi/data/database_schema.py + .sql sources) and server mariadb schema (src/server/db/models.py + migrations); diffs for tables present on both; reports unaligned columns)
tests/scripts/test_schema_diff.py (NEW -- mocks both schemas; asserts diff produces expected output for known drift; asserts clean schemas produce empty diff)
offices/pm/tech_debt/TD-039-us213-migration-gate-no-op-for-drive-summary.md (UPDATE -- mark as resolved with reference to this story's deliverables)
Makefile (OPTIONAL -- if CI hookup is feasible in scope, wire schema_diff.py to make pre-commit; otherwise document manual usage in docs/testing.md)
doNotTouch: Server schema definitions (this story is detection, not modification)

groundingRefs.value: TD-038 (drive_summary schema drift) was silent for weeks because no diff check existed
PRD template addition is cheap, catches at grooming time
CI schema-diff is more reliable, catches at PR time
Both (a) + (c) chosen for layered defense

acceptance: Pre-flight audit: rg `schema_diff|migration_gate|database_schema` to map current state. Record filesActuallyTouched.
offices/pm/prds/_template.md adds 'Server migration required: yes / no / N/A' as a checkbox in the schema-changes section
scripts/schema_diff.py reads Pi schema (sqlite) + server schema (mariadb model + migrations); outputs JSON-formatted diff report
Diff identifies: tables in Pi but not server, tables in server but not Pi, columns in Pi but not server, columns in server but not Pi
test_schema_diff.py: mock both schemas with known drift -> assert diff matches expected; mock clean schemas -> assert diff empty
TD-039 marked resolved with reference to this story
(Optional) Makefile integration: schema_diff runs as part of make pre-commit
Fast suite + new tests pass; ruff clean; sprint_lint 0 errors
verification: python scripts/schema_diff.py (smoke run -- verify produces diff or clean output)
invariants: Detection-only -- this story does NOT auto-generate migrations
Pi schema is authoritative for shared tables; server modernizes to match
Diff output is deterministic (same inputs -> same outputs) for CI compatibility
stopConditions: If parsing migrations to derive server schema is fragile (depends on migration framework specifics) -- STOP, parse from src/server/db/models.py only; document migrations-as-source as future enhancement
If CI hookup requires GitHub Actions changes outside the project's existing workflow -- STOP, document manual usage; CI integration is a future story

stories.title: TD-032 close: delete tiered-battery defined-but-never-called dead code
stories.intent: Per TD-032 (filed Sprint 17): tiered-battery handlers were defined but never wired into AlertManager. Sprint 17 US-223 cleared the dead BatteryMonitor wrong-thresholds class (TD-031), but TD-032's tiered-battery remnant survived. Spool's 2026-04-21 power audit confirmed: no consumer code path exercises these handlers; they're documentation-only. Delete the dead code + delete the test files testing the dead code + sweep imports. Low-risk hygiene story.

filesToTouch: src/pi/alerts/* OR src/common/alerts/* (find tiered-battery handler definitions; rg `tiered.?battery|tieredBattery` to locate)
tests/pi/alerts/* OR tests/common/alerts/* (delete test files testing the dead handlers)
config.json (remove any tieredBattery sections that exist but go unused)
src/common/config/validator.py (remove any tieredBattery DEFAULTS)
offices/pm/tech_debt/TD-032-tiered-battery-defined-never-called.md (UPDATE -- mark resolved with deletion summary)
filesToRead: Sprint 17 US-223 commit + closure note (TD-031 deletion pattern to mirror)
doNotTouch: AlertManager core wiring or other alert handlers

groundingRefs.value: Tiered-battery handlers defined but never called in production
Sprint 17 US-223 mirror pattern (deleted BatteryMonitor class + battery_log table)
Low-risk hygiene -- no production behavior change expected

acceptance: Pre-flight audit: rg `tiered.?battery|tieredBattery` src/ tests/ config.json. Record exact symbols + files. Confirm no live consumer.
Delete tiered-battery handler code + tests + config sections + validator DEFAULTS
Verify AlertManager + adjacent code paths still pass tests after deletion
TD-032 marked resolved with deletion summary
Fast suite passes (no regressions); ruff clean; sprint_lint 0 errors
verification: rg `tiered.?battery|tieredBattery` src/ tests/ config.json (post-deletion -- should return empty)
invariants: No production behavior change -- only deletion of unused code
AlertManager core unchanged
Mirror Sprint 17 US-223 deletion pattern
stopConditions: If audit finds tiered-battery IS being called somewhere -- STOP immediately, file revised TD; do not delete live code
If deletion breaks existing tests (other than the dead-handler tests being deleted) -- STOP, investigate before continuing

stories.title: TD-033 audit: telemetry-logger UPS plumbing -- consolidate or close
stories.intent: Per TD-033 (filed Sprint 16): Spool's audit flagged that telemetry-logger has UPS plumbing whose call sites are unaudited -- unclear whether they fire in production or are dead branches. After Sprint 19's UpsMonitor rebuild (US-235) and Sprint 20's PowerMonitor activation (US-243), the right time to audit. Outcome can be (a) confirmed-live: document the integration in specs/architecture.md; (b) confirmed-dead: delete the plumbing per Sprint 17 US-223 pattern; (c) ambiguous: file follow-up TD with specific audit findings + recommendation. Audit-only story; deletion or doc-update follows from findings.
dependencies: US-243 (PowerMonitor activation may surface live consumers; do US-243 first to avoid false-dead conclusions)

filesToTouch: Findings written to offices/pm/tech_debt/TD-033-telemetry-logger-ups-plumbing-unaudited.md (UPDATE -- audit conclusion: live/dead/ambiguous + path forward)
If LIVE: specs/architecture.md (add documentation of integration)
If DEAD: src/pi/telemetry/* + tests/pi/telemetry/* + config.json (delete; mirror US-250 / US-223 pattern)
If AMBIGUOUS: offices/pm/tech_debt/TD-XXX (NEW follow-up TD with specific audit findings)
doNotTouch: UpsMonitor or PowerMonitor source files (this story is consumer-side audit)

groundingRefs.value: Telemetry-logger UPS plumbing exists but call sites unaudited
Audit must run after US-243 PowerMonitor activation (live consumers may not be visible until then)
Three possible outcomes: confirmed-live (doc it), confirmed-dead (delete it), ambiguous (file follow-up TD)

acceptance: Pre-flight audit: rg `telemetry.*ups|telemetryLogger.*power|telemetryLogger.*battery` src/ tests/. Map exact symbols + call sites.
Trace each call site: is it invoked from an active code path post-US-243? Document yes/no per call site.
Conclude one of: LIVE / DEAD / AMBIGUOUS based on call-site analysis
If LIVE: specs/architecture.md updated with integration documentation; TD-033 marked resolved
If DEAD: deletion PR mirrors US-250 + Sprint 17 US-223 patterns; TD-033 marked resolved with deletion summary
If AMBIGUOUS: new TD filed with specific findings + recommendation; TD-033 superseded
Fast suite passes (no regressions if deletion path); sprint_lint 0 errors
invariants: Audit-first, action-second -- do not delete or document until findings are clear
If outcome is AMBIGUOUS, escalate via new TD; do not silently leave gap
Mirror existing dead-code-delete patterns (US-223 / US-250) if outcome is DEAD
stopConditions: If audit reveals telemetry-logger UPS plumbing IS the only writer for some critical metric -- STOP, propose preservation + documentation; do NOT delete
If deletion path requires schema changes (e.g., dropping a telemetry table) -- STOP, file follow-up story; this is code-only
